reasoning: TIME evaluates candidates for Person of the Year based on the magnitude of their global impact, for better or worse. Xi Jinping has consistently been shortlisted over the past decade as he continues to centralize power and assert China’s influence globally. However, for 2025, betting markets heavily favor Donald Trump and Elon Musk, and Xi Jinping is consistently viewed as a longshot behind them. The explicit Polymarket price of 3% as of August 21, 2025 stands as an incredibly strong anchor that incorporates all the collective intelligence of bettors globally. Since that date, there have been no major geopolitical earthquakes definitively placing Xi at the center of the global stage over the US election candidates and figures like Trump or Musk. Trump’s return to the presidency makes him the overwhelming favorite. Therefore, giving Xi a very slightly higher probability to account for some fat tail risks between August and December 2025, I estimate his chances at 3.5%.
